question.text: Under SEBI (Intermediaries) Regulations, 2008, when can an intermediary choose not to appoint a compliance officer?

answer.text: When the intermediary is registered with only one stock exchange
When the intermediary's annual turnover is below Rs. 1 crore
When it is not carrying on the activity of the intermediary
When the intermediary has fewer than 10 employees

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The regulations provide that the intermediary may not appoint a compliance officer if it is not carrying on the activity of the intermediary. **The Trap:** Size-based exemptions (employee count, turnover) seem logical but the regulation only exempts non-operational intermediaries. **Cross-Exam Context:** Compliance officer appointment requirements are covered in NISM Series III-A and III-B. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** If your intermediary is operational in any capacity, a compliance officer must be appointed - there is no size-based exemption.
